narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings, especially after all the effort you've put into your law school dreams, and I want to walk you through why our prediction model saw your path to the bar exam as riskier than we'd hoped, landing at a 43% chance of not passing. Picture your journey like a story the model pieced together from key chapters of your background: the strongest thread was your Year 3 decile of 4.0, which stood out as the biggest signal pulling toward challenges ahead, more than anything else in your profile. Right behind that was your Year 1 decile of 4.0, reinforcing a pattern of mid-pack performance in a competitive law school environment that made the model worry about building momentum. Your LSAT score of 30.0 added to that cautious picture, suggesting the analytical edge needed for bar success might be a stretch. Even your middle family income bracket played a role, as the model links it to added pressures that sometimes tip the scales toward struggle. Your background as a Black female student was another factor the model weighed, noting historical hurdles others in similar positions have faced. Your undergraduate GPA of 2.9 and full-time study plans rounded out the influences, each nudging the story toward a tougher road. It's not that any one piece defined you—it's how they wove together into a narrative of steady but not standout progress, one our model has seen falter before. This isn't the end of your story, though; many turn these early signals into triumphs by targeting weak spots like grades or test prep. You've got resilience in your profile already—full-time commitment shows that—and with focused steps, you could rewrite this prediction entirely. Hang in there; your determination is what changes the ending.
